topic: Transplant of honest/bias-aware regression-discontinuity partial identification (Kolesar-Rothe AER'18 discrete running variable; Armstrong-Kolesar optimal bias-aware honest CIs) into LLM-cascade routing: identify the NET escalation policy value V = E[Y(large)-Y(small)-kappa | boundary] of a cascade that escalates query x to the large model iff a learned calibrated confidence score c-hat(x) >= tau-hat. Non-verbatim witness: (1) tau-hat is chosen cost-optimally on the SAME data (UCCI 2605.18796), a SELECTED cutoff, so pointwise-in-cutoff honest RD CIs lose validity -> need post-selection honest bounds uniform over a specified population cutoff-selection functional tau*(P); (2) c-hat is a generated/estimated calibrated score (isotonic steps, mass points) endogenous to Y(small) (the small model's own confidence), breaking the classical continuity-of-CEF-in-running-variable proof; (3) deterministic threshold routing fails backdoor-ATE positivity globally (2605.25998 leaves this open), so only the LOCAL net-value at the boundary is identified - a different functional. Target the honest identified set [L*(M),U*(M)] for the POPULATION net value of the selected routing rule under bounded calibration-CEF curvature M, with critical curvature/cost-slack M* at which 0 enters [L*,U*] (sign of net escalation value loses identification), plus the post-selection-honest estimation rung (bias-aware local-linear endpoint estimator with uniform coverage over the M-class and the cutoff-selection; generated-regressor correction). Target tier: field.

open_problems.open_problem: Post-selection honest partial-identification bounds are missing for the population net value of a same-sample, cost-selected LLM-cascade cutoff.

source_refs.claim: UCCI uses isotonic calibration and selects an escalation threshold by constrained cost minimization, creating the same selected-cutoff routing target.
source_refs.author: Kotte

source_refs.claim: Honest CIs for RD-type linear functionals over convex regression classes cannot be tightened by data-dependent tuning while retaining uniform coverage.
source_refs.author: Armstrong and Kolesar

source_refs.claim: Bandwidth snooping requires adjusted critical values when researchers select among bandwidths, but this covers bandwidth choice rather than cutoff-policy selection.
source_refs.author: Armstrong and Kolesar

source_refs.claim: Post-model-selection CIs can be uniformly valid for selected model-specific targets, but the framework is not an RD endpoint selected by a policy functional.
source_refs.author: Bachoc, Preinerstorfer, and Steinberger

source_refs.excerpt: may be set from a density-discontinuity confidence envelope rather than treated as an exactly identified manipulator share
state the directional-differentiability fallback as a separate open-ended question with active-set switching

open_problems.why_unsolved: The web anchors cover the pieces but not their composition: UCCI selects the routing threshold on the empirical cost-calibration problem; Armstrong-Kolesar honesty protects fixed RD-type functionals or bandwidth snooping, not a discontinuity value evaluated at a selected policy cutoff; post-selection CI theory targets selected linear-model objects rather than nonparametric RD endpoints. The prior-proposal evidence flags exactly the same obstacle: selected active sets and envelope-derived quantities need their own population target and equality/strictness analysis rather than being treated as fixed oracle parameters.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp partial-identification theorem defining tau*(P), V(P)=E[Y_large-Y_small-kappa | c=tau*(P)], and an M-curvature/calibration class, then deriving [L*(M),U*(M)] with coverage-valid post-selection critical values uniform over P and the cutoff-selection functional.

writing_exemplar.why: Its motivation arc is the exact application: calibrated uncertainty, cost-optimal routing, and learned threshold selection.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: LLM cascades trade quality for cost -> calibrated uncertainty makes threshold routing operational -> same-sample cost-optimal threshold selection needs an honest population value bound

method_exemplars.technique: Use the modulus-of-continuity and convex-function-class reduction to turn a smoothness-restricted RD functional into sharp honest endpoint formulas.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired [L*(M),U*(M)] is also an extremal problem for a local linear functional over a curvature-bounded class, with the new complication that the evaluation point is tau*(P).

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt simultaneous Gaussian-process critical values for selection over a continuum of tuning parameters.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Cutoff selection in the cascade is structurally a selection-over-index problem, like bandwidth snooping, but the index is a policy cutoff rather than a bandwidth.

method_exemplars.technique: Use post-selection target bookkeeping: define the selected target first, then prove coverage uniformly over selection procedures.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The selected-cutoff cascade value needs the same discipline of distinguishing the selected population target from a fixed-cutoff oracle target.

open_problems.open_problem: Generated calibrated confidence scores in LLM routing lack an honest RD theory when the running variable is estimated, isotonic, mass-pointed, and endogenous to the small model's output.

source_refs.claim: UCCI maps token-level margin uncertainty to error probability by isotonic regression before threshold routing, making the running variable generated and step-valued.
source_refs.author: Kotte

source_refs.claim: Discrete running variables can make conventional RD CIs fail; guaranteed coverage requires explicit restrictions on the conditional expectation function.
source_refs.author: Kolesar and Rothe

source_refs.claim: Running-variable measurement error can sometimes be ignored only under correct treatment classification and smoothness of potential-outcome conditional means in the observed variable.
source_refs.author: Dong and Kolesar

source_refs.claim: Isotonic recalibration creates stratified, stepwise uncertainty scores and changes calibration-error behavior, matching the mass-point score problem.
source_refs.author: Pernot

source_refs.excerpt: Once the feasible score uses the estimated clipped denominator ... additional centered terms
estimated-clipping instability term absent from the note

open_problems.why_unsolved: Discrete-RD and measurement-error RD handle coarse or mismeasured running variables under exogeneity and smoothness conditions, while UCCI-style routing learns a calibrated score from the small model's own confidence/error process and often induces isotonic flats. Prior CausalSmith reviews point to the same failure mode: feasible estimated scores introduce extra first-order or stochastic terms when the theorem silently reasons with an oracle score. The missing assumption class is therefore not ordinary RD smoothness alone, but smoothness and exogeneity stated for the population calibrated score plus a generated-regressor expansion for c-hat.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sufficient-condition theorem defining the population calibrated score c_P(x), proving when boundary CEF limits in c_P identify the local net value, and adding a sample-split or cross-fit generated-regressor expansion for c-hat with explicit isotonic mass-point and first-stage remainder terms.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames discreteness as a coverage and misspecification problem rather than as a minor implementation detail.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: RD inference often assumes a rich running-variable support -> discrete support breaks conventional clustered fixes -> learned isotonic routing scores require honest generated-score RD conditions

method_exemplars.technique: Carry over CEF-restriction based partial identification and honest CI construction for finite or moderately discrete score supports.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The cascade score is often discrete by construction after isotonic calibration, so the endpoint geometry resembles discrete RD more than continuous RD.

method_exemplars.technique: Use observed-running-variable causal interpretation conditions: correct classification plus smooth potential-outcome means in the observed score.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A learned calibrated score is a generated proxy for the latent routing difficulty, so this paper gives the closest RD template for when proxy-score analysis has causal content.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt orthogonalization and cross-fitting to isolate first-stage score-learning error in the endpoint estimator's linear expansion.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target is causal and the nuisance is learned; cross-fitting is the natural way to keep generated-score error from entering the first-order RD limit.

open_problems.open_problem: Deterministic threshold LLM routing needs a formal local boundary estimand because global backdoor ATE positivity fails away from the selected cutoff.

source_refs.claim: The paper identifies routing decisions as causal questions in LLM development, including whether prompts should be routed to larger or smaller models under cost constraints.
source_refs.author: Frauen et al.

source_refs.claim: RD treatment assignment is deterministic, so global ATE identification requires auxiliary structure beyond the boundary design.
source_refs.author: Feng, Hu, Yang, Li, and Zhou

source_refs.claim: When overlap fails, smoothness can yield sharp partial-identification bounds for policy values through linear programs.
source_refs.author: Khan, Saveski, and Ugander

source_refs.claim: Safe RD policy learning decomposes utility into point-identifiable and unidentifiable components because deterministic cutoffs force extrapolation.
source_refs.author: Zhang, Ben-Michael, and Imai

source_refs.excerpt: the upper theorem carries an unused overlap-decay assumption
conditional feasible upper, not a sharp full-class minimax characterization

open_problems.why_unsolved: Causal-LLM work flags routing as a causal problem but does not pin down the estimand under deterministic routing. RD and beyond-overlap papers show why the global value of switching from small to large is not identified without overlap or extrapolation assumptions, while prior weak-overlap reviews warn that unused or overbroad overlap assumptions make a theorem either redundant or false. The unresolved point is to separate the locally identified boundary net value from any globally extrapolated policy value and state exactly which support conditions are needed near tau*(P).
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An estimand theorem separating global policy value, local boundary net value, and smoothness-extrapolated value, proving local identification under two-sided support near c=tau*(P), deriving sharp bounds outside that local region, and giving a counterexample where the global ATE remains unidentified.

writing_exemplar.why: It supplies the motivating LLM-development causal framing while leaving the formal routing identification problem open.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: LLM deployment decisions are causal -> routing changes both quality and cost under logged policies -> deterministic threshold routing identifies only a local boundary value without stronger support

method_exemplars.technique: Use smoothness-restricted partial-identification bounds for no-overlap regions, including sharp LP/closed-form endpoint arguments.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The cascade has a no-overlap region away from the threshold, so the same smoothness-bounds idea transfers after specializing to RD endpoint geometry.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt the decomposition of utility into identifiable and unidentifiable components plus semiparametric policy-learning inference for the identifiable parts.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Safe RD policy learning faces deterministic cutoffs and policy changes, the closest policy-value analogue to threshold cascade routing.

open_problems.open_problem: The decision-facing critical curvature or cost-slack threshold M* is not characterized for when zero first enters the honest identified set of the selected cascade boundary value.

source_refs.claim: LLM cascade threshold policies have a decision-theoretic cost-quality frontier, but the paper does not add causal partial-identification or honest-RD sensitivity thresholds.
source_refs.author: Bouchard

source_refs.claim: Unified routing/cascading methods optimize model-routing tradeoffs but treat threshold performance as a predictive decision problem rather than an identified causal boundary value.
source_refs.author: Dekoninck, Baader, and Vechev

source_refs.claim: Bias-aware honest nonparametric intervals depend explicitly on smoothness restrictions and bias critical values, suggesting a sensitivity index in M.
source_refs.author: Armstrong and Kolesar

source_refs.excerpt: endpoints are continuous and piecewise rational ... with kinks when the binding constraint switches
the endpoint is not merely a different maximizer ... but a strictly smaller value forced by

open_problems.why_unsolved: LLM cascade papers characterize threshold frontiers and cost-quality tradeoffs, while honest inference papers index validity by smoothness or curvature classes. What is absent is the sign-identification sensitivity object tying them together: the smallest M, calibration misspecification, or cost slack at which the conclusion that escalation has positive local net value ceases to be identified. Prior endpoint proposals show this cannot be assumed smooth or trivial because binding constraints can switch and endpoint geometry can kink.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A monotone endpoint theorem proving L*(M) and U*(M), defining M*=inf{M: 0 in [L*(M),U*(M)]}, characterizing equality and strict-sign regions, and giving a finite discrete-score witness where M* is positive and decision-relevant.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames the exact decision question of whether escalation is worth its cost, which the M* threshold answers under partial identification.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Cascades optimize cost-quality frontiers -> escalation is worthwhile only when benefit exceeds cost -> honest partial ID asks the largest curvature class under which that sign remains certified

method_exemplars.technique: Use bias-aware smoothness-indexed endpoint calculations and critical values to express how intervals widen as M increases.
method_exemplars.why_closest: M* is a decision-facing inversion of the same smoothness-indexed honest interval machinery.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt discrete-support honest interval calculations and score-level variance treatment to estimate the endpoints and their sign threshold.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The selected cascade boundary may sit on a finite or moderate calibrated-score support, exactly the regime where discrete-RD endpoint inference matters.

open_problems.open_problem: A post-selection-honest estimator is missing for bias-aware local-linear endpoint inference with uniform coverage over curvature, selected cutoff, generated score, and discrete mass points.

source_refs.claim: Robust bias-corrected RD intervals address pointwise fixed-cutoff inference, not selected cutoffs with generated discrete scores.
source_refs.author: Calonico, Cattaneo, and Titiunik

source_refs.claim: Optimal bandwidth choice for RBC RD inference handles bias correction but does not by itself make inference honest under selected policy cutoffs and generated scores.
source_refs.author: Calonico, Cattaneo, and Farrell

source_refs.claim: Selection among bandwidths can badly reduce coverage unless critical values are adjusted uniformly over the searched index set.
source_refs.author: Armstrong and Kolesar

source_refs.claim: Running-variable mass points require coverage-guaranteed alternatives to conventional clustered or fixed-smoothness RD intervals.
source_refs.author: Kolesar and Rothe

source_refs.excerpt: Prove a uniform data-only upper theorem ... deriving the clipped-score drift
Conjectures 1 and 2 are the open kernels: they require exact published DML, LRO, and ADML report summands

open_problems.why_unsolved: The estimation literature supplies robust-bias-corrected RD, bias-aware honest intervals, discrete-score safeguards, and bandwidth-snooping adjustments separately. The cascade estimator needs all four at once, plus generated-score nuisance terms. Prior CausalSmith reviews repeatedly rejected high-level oracle claims when primitive uniform conditions, drift terms, or exact variance/report objects were missing, so an acceptable rung must state the estimator and its limit law rather than deferring to standard inference.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A uniform coverage theorem for confidence sets C_n(M) satisfying inf_P Pr_P(V(P) in C_n(M)) >= 1-alpha over the M-class and cutoff-selection rule, with a bias-aware local-linear endpoint estimator, simultaneous selected-cutoff critical value, generated-score correction, and mass-point variance formula.

writing_exemplar.why: Its exposition keeps the focus on honest coverage and bias accounting, which is the right narrative for the estimator rung.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Conventional RD intervals understate bias -> honest CIs account for worst-case smoothness bias -> selected generated cascade cutoffs need uniform honest endpoint coverage

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt local-polynomial endpoint estimation, bias correction, and asymptotic variance derivations.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The estimator is still a boundary local-linear RD estimator, even though the target and validity class are selected and generated.

method_exemplars.technique: Use simultaneous critical values over a searched index set to protect coverage after reporting a selected specification.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The selected tau-hat and bandwidth/cutoff search create the same coverage failure mode as bandwidth snooping.

method_exemplars.technique: Use discrete-running-variable variance and honest coverage logic rather than continuous-score asymptotics alone.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Isotonic calibrated scores create support points and ties, so the mass-point treatment is structurally necessary.

literature_map:
1. Kotte (2026, arXiv, Kotte2026UCCI) is the application anchor: isotonic calibration maps uncertainty to error probability and same-sample constrained cost minimization selects the escalation threshold.
2. Kolesar and Rothe (2018, AER, KolesarRothe2018DiscreteRD) show discrete running variables break common clustered-SE RD fixes and motivate guaranteed-coverage CEF restrictions.
3. Armstrong and Kolesar (2018, Econometrica, ArmstrongKolesar2018OptimalInference) give minimax/honest CI machinery for linear functionals over convex regression classes and warn against data-dependent tightening.
4. Armstrong and Kolesar (2018, ReStud, ArmstrongKolesar2018BandwidthSnooping) solve the narrower problem of bandwidth snooping, a template for selected-cutoff critical values.
5. Bachoc, Preinerstorfer, and Steinberger (2016, arXiv, BachocPreinerstorferSteinberger2016PostSelection) provide uniformly valid post-model-selection CIs but not for nonparametric RD endpoints.
6. Calonico, Cattaneo, and Titiunik/Farrell (2014-2018, Econometrica/Econometrics Journal) give robust-bias-corrected RD inference for fixed cutoffs and exogenous scores.
7. Dong and Kolesar (2021, arXiv, DongKolesar2021RunningVariableMeasurementError) and Li et al. (2019, arXiv, ordinal RD) suggest proxy-score routes but require assumptions not automatic for small-model confidence scores.
8. Pernot (2023, arXiv, Pernot2023IsotonicStratification) documents isotonic recalibration stratification and mass points, matching the calibrated-score discreteness issue.
9. Frauen et al. (2026, arXiv, FrauenEtAl2026CausalLLM) frames LLM routing as a causal problem but does not solve the deterministic-threshold identification problem.
10. Feng et al. (2024, arXiv, FengHuYangLiZhou2024RDAuxiliaryATE) reinforces that deterministic RD assignment blocks global ATE identification without auxiliary structure.
11. Khan, Saveski, and Ugander (2023, arXiv, KhanSaveskiUgander2023OPEBeyondOverlap) show smoothness-based sharp partial-ID bounds when overlap fails.
12. Zhang, Ben-Michael, and Imai (2022, arXiv, ZhangBenMichaelImai2022SafePolicyRD) decomposes RD policy utility into identifiable and unidentifiable components under deterministic cutoffs.
13. Bouchard (2026, arXiv, Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt) and Dekoninck, Baader, and Vechev (2024, arXiv, routing/cascading) characterize cascade cost-quality frontiers without causal honest partial-ID guarantees.
prior_proposal_map:
1. Direct qid pid_cascade_escalation_rd currently has initialized state/pipeline files but no proposal/review signal.
2. The closest RD source is pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1, which supplies sharp endpoint geometry, finite-support witness pressure, and warnings about treating confidence-envelope quantities as identified structural shares.
3. pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v2 supplies the selected/optimized-functional analogy: active-set switching, equality/strictness conditions, and reviewer-prescribed open-ended fallback questions.
4. stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap_v1 supplies the uniform-in-P warning: data-only uniform theorems must derive drift and nuisance terms from primitive conditions.
5. stat_ate_overlap_decay_v1 supplies the generated-score warning: feasible estimated scores create extra centered stochastic terms beyond oracle expressions.
6. eid_dml_orth_uniform_v2 supplies the estimator-report caution: exact variance/report objects and summands must be pinned down before uniform inference claims.
7. pid_multivariate_interval_regression_v1 is a negative endpoint template: frontier pruning and dominance shortcuts fail without sharpness proofs.
8. The supplied study-pipeline gaps were reviewed as augmenting candidates; the LATE/Manski interval-regression items were not emitted because they do not engage this run's RD-to-LLM-cascade transplant witness.
